title: Key Derivation, HKDF, and Key Separation · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Key Separation Tree
summary: A protocol slices one shared secret into keys, uses user passwords as HKDF input, omits transcript context, and changes output length without versioning. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the key separation tree increment in labeled key schedule and derivation tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Derive independent role, direction, epoch, algorithm, and purpose keys from a versioned hierarchy without key cycles.
Apply HKDF extract and expand with supported hashes, salt, labeled info, bounded output, and one-way key schedule ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Key Separation Tree · Theory Crypto Go Key Separation Tree
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5848 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive bounded purpose- and role-separated HKDF keys bound to the negotiated transcript; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive independent role, direction, epoch, algorithm, and purpose keys from a versioned hierarchy without key cycles.
